content.text: @[Darl Anthony Pepito] — This ticket has moved to Backlog and needs t-shirt sizing.
Please update the T-Shirt Size field (XS, S, M, L, XL) at your earliest convenience. Leave unassigned when done. Thanks!
Design Note - SM-1103: Project Cascade Template Management Screen
- Stores template definitions (id, name, is_default, hidden, created, modified, modified_by)
- Stores template steps/records (id, cascade_template_id FK, name, days, sort_order, hidden, created, modified)
- List templates with pagination, sorting, filtering
- List items for a specific template
- Create or update a template item
- Core template model with Hideable behavior, data() method for filtered/sorted/paginated listing
- Template step/record model with Hideable behavior, belongsTo CascadeTemplate
FK (SM-1104), and formula field logic will reference cascade data (SM-1106)
No existing APIs are modified in this ticket
All endpoints are new and follow the established admin CRUD pattern (same as Jobtitles, Capabilities, Divisions controllers)

table: id (PK auto-increment), name (varchar 255, unique), is_default (tinyint default 0 - reserved for SM-1105), hidden (tinyint default 0), created (datetime), modified (datetime), modified_by (int FK users)
table: id (PK auto-increment), cascade_template_id (int FK), name (varchar 255), days (int), sort_order (int), hidden (tinyint default 0), created (datetime), modified (datetime)
Insert admin menu item: INSERT INTO menuitems (name, controller, action, icon, type, spa, mobile, parent_id) VALUES ('Cascade Templates', 'cascade_templates', '', 'account_tree', 'admin', 1, 0, 0)
actsAs: Hideable (soft delete, auto-excludes hidden from queries)
data(page, limit, data) method for paginated listing with filter/sort support
data(template_id) method to list items for a template ordered by sort_order
reorder(items) method to bulk update sort_order values
save() - JSON input, create/update template record, return refreshed list
data() - JSON filters/sort, return paginated template list
items() - Return items for a given template_id
deleteitem() - Soft delete a template item
Follows established admin module pattern (JobtitlesAdmin, CapabilitiesAdmin)
cascade-template-admin.module.ts - imports SharedModule, MaterialModule, FormsModule, ReactiveFormsModule
cascade-template-admin-routing.module.ts - single route to list component
cascade-template-admin.service.ts - extends BaseService, defines tableData columns
pages/list/cascade-template-admin-list.component.ts - uses shared app-list-table for template listing
Reuses shared components: EditDialogComponent (create/edit template name), DeleteDialogComponent (confirm delete), AppListTableComponent (table with search/sort)
Frontend - Template Items Management (nested within template):
When user clicks a template row, expand or navigate to show that template's items
Items list shows: name, days, sort_order with inline or dialog-based editing
Add/edit item dialog with fields: name (required), days (required, numeric)
Drag-and-drop reordering for sort_order or manual number input
Add lazy-loaded route in app.routing.ts: path main/cascade-template-admin
Menu item auto-appears in Admin sidebar via UAC/menuitems system - no frontend menu code changes needed
Using Hideable behavior for both tables to preserve data integrity. Cascade template items linked to projects (in future SM-1104/SM-1106) should not be permanently destroyed.
Added now but unused until SM-1105. This avoids a schema migration later.
New admin menu object admin:Cascade Templates will need to be granted to appropriate roles/subroles in the UAC system.

Unlike simple admin screens (single table), this has a parent-child relationship (template to items). The UI will need a master-detail pattern: template list with expandable/clickable rows showing items.
Template items have a sort_order field for explicit ordering, since the sequence of cascade steps matters for downstream formula calculations (SM-1106).
*LOW* â€” All changes are new files (3 backend, 12 frontend) plus one 4-line addition to app.routing.ts. No existing code was modified. The new SQL migration uses NOT EXISTS guards for safe re-runs.

Clean implementation of a master-detail CRUD admin screen for cascade templates and their items. The code follows established patterns closely â€” the backend controller and models mirror the Jobtitles module, and the frontend service properly extends BaseService. The parent-child relationship (templates â†’ items) is well-handled with BehaviorSubjects for item state management and sort-order reordering. All 5 acceptance criteria are met. A few minor issues noted below, none blocking.

* *[WARNING]* â€” cascade_templates_controller.php:34 â€” Double read of php://input in save(). The input stream is consumed on line 14 and read again on line 34, returning empty string. Effect is benign (returns unfiltered list after save). Pattern copied from Jobtitles controller, so consistent.

* *[WARNING]* â€” cascade_template.php / cascade_template_item.php â€” utf8_encode() is deprecated in PHP 8.2+. Safe on current PHP 7.4 runtime. Systemic issue across all models, not specific to this ticket.

* *[SUGGESTION]* â€” cascade_template_item.php:reorder() â€” Issues one saveField() query per item. Fine for typical sizes (5-20 items). A single UPDATE with CASE/WHEN would be more efficient at scale.
* *[SUGGESTION]* â€” list.component.scss â€” ::ng-deep is deprecated in Angular. Still works in Angular 11. Consistent with existing codebase patterns.
* *[SUGGESTION]* â€” Service has both providedIn:'root' and module providers entry. Redundant but consistent with Jobtitles pattern.
* *[SUGGESTION]* â€” No pagination controls in template list. Matches Jobtitles which also lacks pagination UI. Worth adding if template count grows.
* (/) Inputs validated and outputs escaped per app conventions
* (/) Edge cases and failure paths handled

Implemented a dedicated admin management screen for project cascade templates. Each template has a name and contains multiple items/steps, each with a configurable number of days. The screen uses a master-detail layout: template list on the left, item management panel on the right.
New controller: cascade_templates_controller.php — CRUD for templates and items (save, data, delete, items, saveitem, deleteitem, reorderitems)
New model: cascade_template.php — Template model with Hideable behavior, paginated data method with filter/sort support
New model: cascade_template_item.php — Item model with Hideable behavior, sort_order reordering support
SQL migration: 20260410_sm1103_cascade_templates.sql — Creates cascade_templates and cascade_template_items tables, adds menu item and UAC permission
Frontend (Git: SM-1103 branch, commit 87cb7317, merged to develop):
New lazy-loaded module: cascade-template-admin with routing, service extending BaseService
List page: master-detail layout with template table (search, sort, add/edit/delete) and item panel
Items component: table with name, days, sort order (move up/down), add/edit/delete
Item dialog: reactive form with name (required) and days (required, min 0, whole number)
Run the SQL migration (20260410_sm1103_cascade_templates.sql) on the database. Ensure the admin user has the "Cascade Templates" permission.
— Click "Cascade Templates" in the admin sidebar menu. Verify the management screen loads with an empty template list.
— Click "Add New", enter a name (e.g., "Site Launch"), save. Verify it appears in the list.
— Click the three-dot menu on a template, select Edit, change the name, save. Verify the name updates.

— Click a template row to select it. In the right panel, click "Add Item". Enter name "Design" and days "5", save. Add more items ("Procurement" - 10 days, "Installation" - 7 days). Verify all items appear with correct values.
— Use the up/down arrow buttons to reorder items. Verify the order persists after page refresh.
— Edit an item's name or days. Delete an item. Verify changes persist.
— Delete a template via the three-dot menu. Confirm deletion. Verify the template is removed from the list.
Frontend Git: SM-1103 branch, commit 87cb7317, merged to develop

regarding SM-1103 (Cascade Template Management Screen) — the ticket title says "Project Tracker - Cascade Template Management Screen." Currently, we've placed "Cascade Templates" as a standalone item in the Admin menu, similar to how "Projects Admin" is separate from "Project Tracker."

Should "Cascade Templates" stay as its own admin menu item, or should it be placed somewhere within the Project Tracker section? If so, where exactly — as a sub-item under Project Tracker, or accessible from within the Project Tracker screen itself?
Run the SQL migration (20260410_sm1103_cascade_templates.sql) on the database. Ensure the admin user has the "Cascade Templates" permission.
— Click "Cascade Templates" in the admin sidebar menu. Verify the management screen loads with an empty template list.
— Click "Add New", enter a name (e.g., "Site Launch"), save. Verify it appears in the list.
— Click the three-dot menu on a template, select Edit, change the name, save. Verify the name updates.

— Click a template row to select it. In the right panel, click "Add Item". Enter name "Design" and days "5", save. Add more items ("Procurement" - 10 days, "Installation" - 7 days). Verify all items appear with correct values.
— Use the up/down arrow buttons to reorder items. Verify the order persists after page refresh.
— Edit an item's name or days. Delete an item. Verify changes persist.
— Delete a template via the three-dot menu. Confirm deletion. Verify the template is removed from the list.

— Click "Cascade Templates" in the admin sidebar menu. Verified the management screen loads with an empty template list.
— Click "Add New", enter a name (e.g., "Site Launch"), save. Verified it appears in the list.

— Use the up/down arrow buttons to reorder items. Verified the order persists after page refresh.
— Edit an item's name or days. Delete an item. Verified changes persist.
— Delete a template via the three-dot menu. Confirm deletion. Verified the template is removed from the list.
